summary: Blessing Mouth is a freemium faith-based voice declaration app where Christian believers browse a library of scripture-rooted declarations, record themselves speaking God's Word aloud over their own lives, and build a daily practice through customizable reminders. It serves believers who feel spiritually stuck despite church, Bible reading, and serving, by turning head knowledge into a consistent embodied practice grounded in Joshua 1:8. Revenue is recurring via Silver/Bronze/Gold subscriptions ($2.99/mo or $24/yr) layered over a permanent free tier.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Get the new free signup to record and play back their first declaration within 24–48 hours — the activation event the entire product value depends on.
campaigns.trigger: Account created (free email or Google signup); email 1 sends within seconds of signup.
campaigns.segment: All new signups; later branch on whether they recorded and enabled a reminder.
campaigns.rationale: Blessing Mouth's value is entirely habit-dependent — users who never make a first recording disengage before the aha moment, making activation the single highest-leverage flow for this brand.

emails.purpose: Warm welcome, set the daily-rhythm expectation, and drive the one action that creates first value: record and play back a declaration.
emails.subject: Welcome — your first declaration is waiting
emails.preview: Two minutes to hear God's Word in your own voice.
keyPoints: Personal, pastoral welcome from the founder — you're here because something felt missing despite doing everything right
The one first action: pick a theme, record one declaration, and play it back — most people feel the shift on the first listen
Set the expectation early: turn on a daily reminder so the practice becomes a rhythm, not a one-time visit
Hit reply anytime — a real person reads every message

campaigns.name: Free-to-Paid Conversion (Silver)
campaigns.objective: Convert an activated free user into a paying Silver subscriber by connecting the difference they've already felt to the depth the full library unlocks.
campaigns.trigger: First recording completed OR ~7 days of free usage; behavioral nudge fires once the user has felt the practice work.
campaigns.segment: Activated free users (made at least one recording); branch by engagement depth.
campaigns.rationale: The brand's own instruction is 'upgrade only when you feel the difference,' so the core revenue email must arrive right after activation and trade on lived experience, not discounts.

emails.purpose: Reference the difference they've already felt and present Silver as the way to bring that practice to every area of life.
emails.subject: You felt it on the free plan. Here's what's behind the next door.
emails.preview: Every theme — healing, provision, identity, peace — unlimited.
keyPoints: Name what they've experienced: hearing your own voice declare the Word changes how your spirit receives it
Free gave you a taste — Silver opens every theme, the full library, unlimited recordings, and custom lists and reminders
Just $2.99/mo, or $24/yr to save 33% — cancel anytime, no pressure
Frame the cost against the area of life you're believing for — this is the practice working for all of it, every day

campaigns.name: Lead-Magnet Nurture (Joshua 1:8 Premise)
campaigns.objective: Move free users who haven't activated toward the practice by dismantling the #1 objection — 'I already read my Bible / go to church' — with the brand's core theological premise.
campaigns.trigger: Free signup who has not yet made a recording; drip spaced over the consideration window.
campaigns.segment: Non-activated free users; branch by the declaration theme they browsed first.
campaigns.rationale: The entire offer rests on reading ≠ speaking, and the objection-handling email is the one send that reframes the prospect's whole relationship to the practice — the highest-value piece of the nurture for this brand.

emails.purpose: Handle the top objection by teaching that Joshua 1:8 attaches the promise to speaking (hagah), not silent reading — then invite the first recording.
emails.subject: "But I already read my Bible"
emails.preview: Joshua 1:8 ties the promise to your mouth, not your eyes.
keyPoints: Meet the objection head-on with warmth — this is exactly what most believers were never taught
Joshua 1:8's 'hagah' means to murmur, mutter, speak aloud — the promise of success is conditioned on declaring, not just knowing
Reading informs the mind; speaking trains the spirit — and every silent day leaves the voice of doubt unopposed
Try it once: pick one verse and say it out loud over your own life today

campaigns.name: Win-Back / Re-Engagement
campaigns.objective: Reactivate free and paid users who have gone quiet by restarting the daily declaration rhythm before they fully drop off.
campaigns.trigger: No app open / no recording for 7–14 days.
campaigns.segment: Dormant users; branch dormant free vs. lapsing paid; sunset non-responders after the sequence.
campaigns.rationale: The brand's own line — 'every day you don't declare, that other voice gets another unopposed rep' — maps perfectly onto win-back and reframes silence as a battle being lost, not just a missed habit.

emails.purpose: Re-open the relationship with the unopposed-voice framing and make the return a single, low-friction recording.
emails.subject: Every silent day, that other voice gets a free rep
emails.preview: We saved your declarations — pick up right where you left off.
keyPoints: No shame, no guilt — life gets loud and the practice slips; that's exactly when it matters most
When you go quiet, the voice of doubt and unworthiness keeps speaking, unopposed
One recording is all it takes to restart the rhythm — your lists and reminders are still here
Re-anchor on the outcome they came for: peace and identity that move from doctrine into daily reality

campaigns.name: Value Newsletter (Weekly Declaration)
campaigns.objective: Stay top-of-mind between app sessions and reinforce the speaking habit with one scripture-rooted declaration each week.
campaigns.segment: All engaged free and paid users; suppress active onboarding and route dormant users to Win-Back.
campaigns.rationale: An always-on weekly devotional keeps the practice present between sessions and builds the habit loop the product's retention depends on, without ever leaning on discounts.

emails.purpose: Deliver one verse and theme for the week with a short reflection, and prompt the reader to record it — the recurring 'This week's declaration' habit anchor.
emails.subject: This week's declaration to speak over your life
emails.preview: One verse. Say it out loud before the week gets loud.
keyPoints: Recurring 'This week's declaration' section — one scripture and life theme per send to build a weekly habit
A short, pastoral reflection on why speaking this specific verse aloud matters right now
80% value / 20% soft nudge — single primary CTA to open the app and record it
Reinforce the core practice: the Word lands differently when it's in your own voice
